mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive revisions in earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 returns higher over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth is a widely-watched coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the rank correlation between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3.5%, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. Higher prospective interest rates increase the discount rate on future earnings, causing a repricing of equities downward.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has a dual mandate including price stability.
failure_condition: The mechanism is rejected if, during periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower than the average return when the condition is not met.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a fall in real retail sales (RSAFS, adjusted for CPIAUCSL) signals a severe contraction in consumer health. This joint signal precedes sharp downward revisions to revenue forecasts, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if a strategy going short the S&P 500 when the trigger is active does not produce a statistically significant positive alpha over the 2000-2012 period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that supply chains are healing and output is rising to meet recovering demand. This signals high operating leverage and margin expansion, which is bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key input for the NBER business cycle dating committee.
failure_condition: The mechanism is falsified if the signal's positive performance is not statistically concentrated in the 24 months following an NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below a very low threshold, such as 4.5%, it signals a late-cycle economy with a tight labor market. This leads to wage inflation, corporate margin compression, and pre-emptive Fed tightening, all of which are negative for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Late 1990s market peak was preceded by very low unemployment.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not negative and statistically significant in periods when UNRATE is below 4.5% for at least 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer confidence and have a large positive multiplier effect on the economy through construction jobs and demand for materials and furnishings. This broad-based demand signal drives equity prices higher over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly cyclical, interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the information coefficient of 3-month changes in HOUST predicting 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.03 in rolling 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) occurring while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating indicates a monetary policy error of tightening into weakness. This interaction is highly bearish for equities as it raises the probability of a recession.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is decelerating
evidence_refs: fact:Several recessions, including 1981-82, were associated with Fed tightening.
failure_condition: The mechanism is rejected if the S&P 500 has positive average 60-day returns in more than 40% of the instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, short-term increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a high-frequency signal of an impending economic downturn, causing an immediate negative reaction in equity markets as investors price in lower growth.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Sahm Rule' uses a rising unemployment rate as a real-time recession indicator.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the correlation between the 1-month change in UNRATE and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significantly negative.
